contents.summary: As a Main Admin, I want Ask AI Alpha access controlled by a feature-flag and permission check so only Main Admins can access Alpha features
contents.description:
As a Main Admin / I want the Ask AI Alpha feature to be visible and usable only when a tenant-level feature-flag is ON and the user has the Main Admin role / So that access is restricted during Alpha and dependent work can safely be developed against a stable access contract.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Given the feature-flag is OFF, the 'AI Assist' button is hidden for all users including Main Admins.
- Given the feature-flag is ON and the user is a Main Admin, the 'AI Assist' button is visible and usable.
- Given the feature-flag is ON and the user is not a Main Admin, the 'AI Assist' button is hidden.
- The feature-flag and role-check are exposed via a simple backend API or client-side feature contract for other teams to read.

contents.summary: As a user, I want the 'Limit to current view' search scope control to work with feature-flagged Ask AI so the search-scope UI can be built independently
contents.description:
As a user / I want a 'Limit to current view' checkbox (default ON) under the question field that controls whether the query is scoped to the current location or the whole platform / So that Ask AI results respect the selected scope.

Acceptance Criteria:
- The checkbox is present, defaulted to ON when the Ask AI panel opens.
- The UI persists the checkbox state while the panel remains open.
- If the Ask AI access feature-flag is OFF, the entire Ask AI panel is hidden; implementation uses the access-control contract (from IB2-318 slice).
- The control is implemented against a mocked Ask AI client so it can be validated without the full backend AI service.

contents.summary: As a user, I want the Ask button UI and keyboard behaviour implemented so I can trigger searches in the panel independently
contents.description:
As a user / I want an 'Ask' button under the question field that is disabled by default and enables when a valid question is entered; I want hover text 'Click or hit enter to ask your question.' when enabled / So that I can trigger AI searches from the UI.

Acceptance Criteria:
- The Ask button is present and disabled when panel opens.
- When input meets basic validation (non-empty, > X chars), the Ask button becomes enabled and shows hover text 'Click or hit enter to ask your question.'
- Pressing Enter triggers the same action as clicking the enabled button.
- The Ask button is only rendered when the Ask AI access contract indicates the user can see the feature (feature-flag + role check). A mocked permission API is used for local testing.

contents.summary: As a Main Admin, I want the Ask button to respect permission and the 'Limit to current view' control so the Ask action uses UI state and access contract
contents.description:
As a Main Admin / I want the Ask button to appear only after Main Admin visibility rules are applied and to use the 'Limit to current view' checkbox state when invoking queries / So that authorized users can perform scoped searches and the integration point with Ask AI is well-defined.

Acceptance Criteria:
- The Ask button only renders if the access-contract (feature-flag + Main Admin role) allows it.
- When clicked, the Ask action sends the checkbox state (limitToCurrentView: true/false) to the mocked AI client.
- The Ask button remains disabled until the question input is valid.
- The story is implemented against mocked endpoints (permission check + AI client) to allow independent development.

contents.summary: As a Public user, I want the public upload header simplified (hide Database name and close icon) so the public upload page has a streamlined header
contents.description:
As a Public user / I want the public upload page header to omit the Database name and close ('x') icon when creating a public record / So that the public upload page presents a simplified layout required by downstream styling and success-state changes.

Acceptance Criteria:
- When the current user is unauthenticated public uploader, the page header only displays "Create {{Record}}" (no Database name) and no 'x' close icon.
- The simplified header is controlled by a feature-flag to allow staged rollout.
- The simplified header is implemented and available for the Guest Upload success-state story to reference.

contents.summary: As a Guest user, I want a consistent success confirmation presentation that respects the simplified header flag and uses a small set of approved variants for subtle variety
contents.description:
In order to maintain a trustworthy and consistent UX, As a Guest user I want the submission success confirmation to present one of a small, approved set of visual variants (e.g., two variants) for icon size and layout while keeping the main text 'Your {{Record}} has been submitted'. Behavior must degrade gracefully based on the simplified public header feature-flag: if the flag is OFF, use the existing header layout with no variants; if ON, apply one of the approved variants deterministically (round-robin or tenant-seeded) rather than purely random. Measurable constraint: maximum of two approved variants for MVP to avoid UX inconsistency.

Acceptance Criteria (binary Gherkin):

Scenario 1 — Variant selection when feature-flag ON
Given simplified public header feature-flag = ON
When a Guest completes a submission
Then the success confirmation uses one of the two approved variants and displays text 'Your {{Record}} has been submitted'

Scenario 2 — Deterministic selection
Given multiple submissions from the same tenant
When successive confirmations are shown
Then variant selection follows a deterministic pattern (round-robin or tenant-seeded) rather than pure randomness

Scenario 3 — Graceful fallback when flag OFF
Given simplified public header feature-flag = OFF
When a Guest completes a submission
Then the system falls back to the existing header layout with no variant presentation and no visual regressions

Scenario 4 — Toggleable presentation component
Given the implementation uses a presentation component
When administrators toggle the feature-flag
Then the component respects the flag and can be switched off without code changes

contents.summary: As a client, I want an agreed Smart Pages 'List' API contract and a mocked endpoint so the UI can be developed independently
contents.description:
As a developer / I want a documented REST contract for the Smart Pages list API and a mocked endpoint returning representative results / So that frontend teams can implement the Smart Pages list UI without waiting for full backend completion.

Acceptance Criteria:
- A Swagger/OpenAPI contract for GET /smart-pages is published describing IDs, titles, pagination, and minimal metadata.
- A mocked endpoint (or mock server) returns sample payloads matching the contract.
- The mock supports edge cases: empty list, large page, and permission-limited responses.

contents.summary: As a user, I want the Status column displayed with updated chip colors so the Workflow Request list shows current statuses
contents.description:
As a user / I want a 'Status' column displayed to the right of Request Details showing a colored chip for the current workflow request status (Pending, Approved, Declined, Reviewed, Cancelled) / So that I can quickly scan request states in the list.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Status column renders in the specified position with chips for Pending, Approved, Declined, Reviewed, Cancelled.
- Chip colors follow the provided designs for this view; a note documents other locations that will require color updates later.
- Existing cancellation logic and derived statuses are respected; sample data demonstrates typical scenarios.

contents.summary: As a user, I want to view Pages and specific Stages on the record view so I can navigate records and staged content
contents.description:
As a user / I want navigation to single-record pages and staged records such that Stage 1 is viewable by default and future stages are shown but disabled when pending / So that users can navigate to pages and understand stage visibility constraints.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Single-record views allow navigation to any Page/section.
- For staged records: when Stage 1 is pending, Stage 1 is viewable and future stage names appear but are disabled.
- Navigation respects stage context and updates the visible page content accordingly.
- The implementation exposes a simple contract for which stage is active so other stories (e.g., status display) can integrate.

contents.summary: As a user, I want a Request Status section in the Record Publish Approval page that shows static status info so request details are available without full stage-navigation integration
contents.description:
As a user / I want a 'Request Status' section showing Request Status, Approvals Still Required, and Waiting On for the currently viewed page or stage (static for now) / So that reviewers can see the essential approval information even before dynamic stage navigation is integrated.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Request Status section shows Request Status, Approvals Still Required, and Waiting On.
- When viewing staged records, the section shows information for the currently-selected stage; if dynamic stage navigation is not available, a static stage selector input is used for acceptance testing.
- Data rendering uses a small contract/mock API so the component can be validated independently.

contents.summary: As an Admin, I want an 'Enable Azure Image Tagging' MCP setting so Azure tagging can be toggled per tenant
contents.description:
As an Admin / I want a new MCP setting 'Enable Image Tagging (Azure)' under Asset Intelligence to control whether Azure is used for image tagging / So that tenant admins can opt into Azure image-tagging.

Acceptance Criteria:
- New setting exists in Admin > Asset Intelligence: 'Enable Image Tagging (Azure)'.
- When ON, the system routes image-tagging requests to Azure by config; when OFF, Imagga remains active.
- The setting is stored in the MCP settings table and exposed via the admin API.

contents.summary: As a Platform Engineer, I want Azure provider precedence and Imagga-disable behaviour so the runtime uses Azure when enabled
contents.description:
As an engineer / I want provider selection logic that prefers Azure when 'Enable Image Tagging (Azure)' is ON and disables Imagga for image tagging / So that tagging requests are routed consistently.

Acceptance Criteria:
- A provider-selection component selects Azure for image-tagging calls when setting is ON.
- Imagga calls are skipped/disabled for image-tagging when Azure is enabled.
- Unit tests cover provider selection logic.

contents.summary: As a Billing engineer, I want 'Azure Image Processing Limit (Images)' and renaming of Azure Video limit to minutes so quotas and billing are tracked correctly
contents.description:
As an Admin/Billing user / I want a new MCP setting 'Azure Image Processing Limit (Images) (per billing cycle)' and to rename 'Azure Video Processing Limit' to use minutes / So that quotas and billing for Azure media processing are explicit and measurable.

Acceptance Criteria:
- New setting added and exposed in admin UI and API.
- Azure Video Processing Limit label updated to clarify units (minutes) in UI and API.
- Quota values persist and are included in billing calculations (mock billing hook provided for initial validation).

contents.summary: As a Data Engineer, I want to migrate Imagga image-tagging configurations and usage history to Azure so historical tagging data is preserved
contents.description:
As a Data Engineer / I want a migration plan and migration job that moves Imagga tagging configurations and usage history into Azure-compatible storage / So that legacy tagging data is available under the new Azure provider.

Acceptance Criteria:
- A documented migration plan exists with sample transforms.
- A runnable migration job migrates a subset of data as a smoke-test and produces verification reports.
- Migration supports rollback and idempotency for repeated runs.

contents.summary: As a Product Owner, I want Azure image-tagging analytics, reporting, and notifications so usage and limits are monitored
contents.description:
As a Product Owner / I want analytics dashboards and notifications for Azure image processing usage, provider failures, and quota exhaustion / So that admins and operators can monitor health and usage.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Basic analytics metrics collected: images processed, errors, avg latency per billing cycle.
- Notifications are triggered when processing usage approaches configured quota and when provider errors exceed threshold.
- A simple reporting endpoint provides usage by tenant for the billing cycle.

contents.summary: As a Doc Writer, I want updated APIs, permissions, and rollout documentation for Azure image-tagging so teams can integrate and operate the new provider
contents.description:
As a Doc Writer / I want clear documentation of new APIs, MCP settings, permission requirements, and rollout steps / So that internal teams can integrate and operations can support the feature.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Documentation pages covering MCP settings, provider selection behavior, quota endpoints, and migration notes are published.
- API changes and permission requirements are documented with examples.
- Rollout checklist for staged tenant enablement is provided.

contents.summary: As a Reporting user, I want the weekly report renamed and to include Question/Answer/Rating data so reports reflect Asset Intelligence metrics
contents.description:
As a Reporting user / I want update to the weekly report to include Question/Answer/Rating data and to rename the report to 'weekly_asset_intelligence_report' / So that the report surface includes Asset Intelligence metrics.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Report schema updated to include Q/A/Rating fields.
- Report file/name changed to weekly_asset_intelligence_report.
- Smoke tests validate the new fields are exported in weekly runs.

contents.summary: As a Reporting engineer, I want to remove Dev/Non-Prod server entries from the weekly report so only production environments remain
contents.description:
As a Reporting engineer / I want to exclude known dev/test server identifiers from the weekly report exports / So that production reports are not polluted with development environments.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Configurable exclusion list applied to report generation (list includes provided dev/devtest hosts).
- Tests demonstrate excluded dev hosts are removed from sample exports.

contents.summary: As a Data Engineer, I want a historical data migration plan to backfill Q/A/Rating into the new report schema
contents.description:
As a Data Engineer / I want a documented migration plan and backfill job to populate historical Q/A/Rating data into the new report store / So that historical reporting remains accurate.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Migration plan documents data sources, transforms, windowing, and validation checks.
- A backfill job runs on a sample historical window and produces validation artifacts.
- Migration is idempotent and can be resumed after failures.

contents.summary: As an Ops engineer, I want automated export/report synchronization and backward compatibility tests so reporting integrations remain stable
contents.description:
As an Ops engineer / I want automation that syncs updated reports to downstream systems and test suites that validate backward compatibility / So that changes to the report schema do not break consumers.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Automation pipeline syncs new report files to a test downstream consumer.
- Backward compatibility tests compare previous schema expectations and flag breaking changes.

contents.summary: As a Page Builder user, I want the ability to move a sub-page to a standalone page (core move) so I can reorganize content
contents.description:
As a Page Builder user / I want a drag-and-drop action to convert a sub-page into a standalone parent page and update navigation order and hierarchy in the editing Navigation Panel / So that I can restructure content quickly.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Drag-and-drop action converts a sub-page into a standalone page and updates the navigation panel order.
- The new page appears in the navigation hierarchy immediately in editor and persists after save.
- Basic migration of child references is performed: direct internal links on the moved page remain functional (deep migrations reserved for subsequent stories).

contents.summary: As an SEO/UX lead, I want updated URLs, breadcrumbs and SEO metadata after a page hierarchy change so external links and navigation remain correct
contents.description:
As an SEO/UX lead / I want the system to update page URLs, breadcrumbs and SEO metadata when a sub-page becomes standalone / So that SEO and user navigation are preserved.

Acceptance Criteria:
- URL rewrite rules or redirects are created for moved pages to preserve inbound links.
- Breadcrumbs reflect the new page hierarchy immediately after move.
- SEO metadata is copied/updated for the new standalone page and redirects added for old URLs.

contents.summary: As an Editor, I want undo/redo and version history for page hierarchy changes so I can revert erroneous moves
contents.description:
As an Editor / I want undo/redo and a versioned history entry for page hierarchy changes including moves / So that accidental changes can be reversed and change history audited.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Move operations create a version entry capturing the prior and new parent/hierarchy.
- Undo reverts a recent move in the editor session; redo reapplies it.
- Version history shows who performed the move and when.

contents.summary: As a Platform Admin, I want audit logging, notifications and permission handling when a page hierarchy changes so stakeholders are informed and access remains correct
contents.description:
As a Platform Admin / I want changes to hierarchy to generate audit logs, notify impacted users, and trigger permission migrations according to rules / So that governance and security are maintained after structural changes.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Move operations emit audit log entries with before/after state.
- Notifications are sent to page owners/editors as per configurable rules (sample implementation for notification).
- Permission propagation for immediate children is applied per a defined rule-set (detailed permission inheritance covered in a follow-up story).

contents.summary: As an Admin, I want to view and configure sub-page permissions in Admin -> Group so I can manage per-page access in line with folder-style UX
contents.description:
As an Admin / I want an admin UI to list sub-pages under a parent and set permission entries (read/edit) inline to match resource folder/sub-folder design / So that admins can manage sub-page permissions directly.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Admin UI shows sub-pages in a hierarchical list and allows direct permission edits per sub-page.
- Changes persist via the permissions API and are reflected in a permissions preview panel.
- Basic permission propagation toggle is available but detailed propagation rules implemented in follow-up stories.

contents.summary: As a Platform Engineer, I want permission propagation logic for sub-pages so permission changes can be propagated like resource folders
contents.description:
As a Platform Engineer / I want a server-side propagation routine to propagate permission changes from parent to children according to configured rules / So that sub-page permission updates can mirror folder-like behavior.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Server-side routine propagates permissions for a configured depth (initially 1 level) and logs propagated changes.
- Tests demonstrate propagation for sample hierarchies.
- Propagation is idempotent and configurable.

contents.summary: As an Admin, I want permission change audit history for sub-pages so I can track who changed access and when
contents.description:
As an Admin / I want changes to sub-page permissions to be recorded in an audit trail with timestamp, actor, and before/after states / So that changes are traceable for compliance.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Permission changes create audit entries with actor, timestamp, resource, and before/after permission set.
- Audit entries are queryable in Admin UI for recent changes.

contents.summary: As an Admin, I want requester email templates to follow a clear inheritance model (global -> tenant -> workflow) so tenants can customize notifications without duplicating global content
contents.description:
In order to let tenants customize requester emails safely, As an Admin, I want the template engine to support a simple fallback inheritance model: workflow-level -> tenant-level -> global-default. The UI should display the effective template and provide an edit option scoped to tenant-level. MVP scope: view effective template, edit tenant-level override, and validation of placeholder tokens.

Acceptance Criteria (binary Gherkin):

Scenario 1 — Fallback resolution
Given a workflow has no workflow-level requester template
And a tenant-level template exists
When evaluating the workflow's effective requester template
Then the system resolves to the tenant-level template

Scenario 2 — UI shows effective template and tenant edit
Given I am an Admin viewing a workflow's requester email template
When I open the template UI
Then it shows the effective rendered template and an "Edit at tenant scope" action when tenant-level override is available

Scenario 3 — Placeholder validation
Given I save a tenant-level override with placeholders
When I submit the change
Then the system validates required placeholders and rejects invalid changes with a clear error

contents.summary: As an Admin, I want requester emails to support localized template variants so recipient locale determines the email language
contents.description:
In order to send localized notifications, As an Admin I want the template system to support language variants with locale fallback. MVP scope: support en/fr/es localized variants for requester emails and a fallback to global-default when a locale variant is missing.

Acceptance Criteria (binary Gherkin):

Scenario 1 — Locale-specific rendering
Given a requester has locale = fr
And a French (fr) template variant exists for the workflow
When the requester notification is generated
Then the rendered email uses the fr template and resolves placeholders correctly

Scenario 2 — Fallback when variant missing
Given a requester has locale = es
And no es template exists for the workflow but global-default exists
When generating the email
Then the system falls back to the workflow-level or tenant/global default template

Scenario 3 — Sample templates render
Given the sample en/fr/es templates provided
When the template renderer runs with sample placeholders
Then each variant renders without unresolved placeholders

contents.summary: As an Ops engineer, I want outgoing requester emails to emit delivery events and a simple dashboard so I can monitor recent failures
contents.description:
In order to monitor notification health, As an Ops engineer I want the outbound email pipeline to record delivery events (sent, delivered, failed) and surface a simple dashboard showing counts for the last 24 hours. MVP scope: capture events from the configured outbound provider (or simulated provider in staging), persist recent 24-hour history, and expose a dashboard with counts and basic error codes for failed deliveries.

Acceptance Criteria (binary Gherkin):

Scenario 1 — Event capture
Given an email provider returns delivery events (sent/delivered/failed)
When the system receives the event
Then the event is stored in the delivery events store with timestamp and status

Scenario 2 — Dashboard counts
Given events exist in the last 24 hours
When I open the Delivery Dashboard
Then it shows counts of sent/delivered/failed for the last 24 hours

Scenario 3 — Failure details
Given a failed delivery event exists
When I inspect the failure on the dashboard
Then I see the error code and provider message for troubleshooting

contents.summary: As an Integrator, I want a pluggable adapter interface and a sample adapter so notification payloads can be forwarded to external providers
contents.description:
In order to integrate tenant-specific delivery pipelines and legacy systems, As an Integrator I want a defined adapter interface and one sample adapter implementation for a single external provider (MVP). The story includes interface design, one adapter implementation, integration test(s) demonstrating payload forwarding, and documentation on adding additional adapters.

Acceptance Criteria (binary Gherkin):

Scenario 1 — Adapter interface defined
Given the notification subsystem design
When reviewing the adapter spec
Then there is a documented interface with required methods and expected payload contract

Scenario 2 — Sample adapter implemented
Given the adapter interface
When the sample adapter is wired in the pipeline
Then notifications are forwarded to the sample external provider and the provider acknowledges receipt in integration tests

Scenario 3 — Documentation
Given the adapter and sample implementation
When a developer reads the README
Then it describes how to implement and register an additional adapter

contents.summary: As a user, I want visual affordances and clear drag-to-position UX for the Image Widget so I can place the focal point visually (with explicit hit targets and persistence)
contents.description:
Scope update (aligns interactions with IB2-308 owner responsibilities):

Clarify hit targets and gestures to remove ambiguity between resizing and focal repositioning:
- Reserve dragging the dotted outline, edge, or explicit resize handles for resizing the widget (this action is owned by IB2-308 and must remain two-way synced with the Nav Width slider).
- Reserve dragging the visible image area (inside the dotted outline) for repositioning the focal point (image translate within the block).
- Provide separate visible affordances/cursors: a resize cursor/handles for outline drag, and a move/hand cursor for image-area drag. Document mobile equivalents (touch-drag on outline/handles = resize; touch-drag on image = focal reposition) and keyboard alternatives (arrow-keys for fine adjust of focal point when image is focused; modifier+arrow for resizing when resize handles focused).

Persistence behavior (align with IB2-308 focal-point persistence service):
- During image-area drag, the UI must update the focal indicator immediately in-memory.
- On drag release, the client MUST persist the focal-point via the shared focal-point persistence API/service owned by IB2-308 (not persist-only to client). Implementers may update a local draft first for instant UX and then call the shared API; however the final persisted value must be stored/retrieved via the shared service so IB2-117 and other consumers reuse the same data.
- Remove the previous acceptance criterion that forbade calling the shared persistence API; instead require that local-draft + shared persistence be supported for snappy UX and global consistency.

Acceptance Criteria (updated):
1) Given an Image Widget placed into a Section — when the widget is placed, then the dotted outline and outer bounding box with 8px padding are visible and follow design tokens.
2) Given an Image Widget on the page — when I drag the image interior (not the outline or handles), then the focal indicator updates immediately in the client and shows the new focal point position.
3) Given a drag action on the image interior — when I release the mouse/touch, then the client persists the focal change through the shared focal-point persistence API/service owned by IB2-308 (a local draft may be used for immediate UX but final storage must call the shared API).
4) Given an Image Widget — when I drag the dotted outline or resize handles, then the widget width changes on the page and resizing behavior is reserved for outline/handles only (not image-area drag); resizing interactions sync with the Nav Width slider as specified in IB2-308.
5) Visual outlines, drag handles, and cursor states follow design tokens and remain visible after resizing operations. Mobile touch gestures and keyboard equivalents for both actions are documented in the UX spec.

Implementation note: Update UI/UX spec and component contract to explicitly name CSS hit areas/selectors for 'image-area' vs 'outline/handles' and define events emitted for each action (e.g., focal:dragStart/dragMove/dragEnd, resize:dragStart/dragMove/dragEnd) so front-end code can route persistence calls correctly.

contents.summary: As a keyboard user, I want keyboard-accessible controls for repositioning the Image Widget so repositioning is accessible and parity with mouse behavior
contents.description:
As a user who relies on keyboard navigation I want to reposition image focal points using keyboard controls (arrow keys, modifiers) so that repositioning produces the same proportional placement results as mouse drag.

Acceptance Criteria:
1) Given focus is on an Image Widget — when I activate keyboard reposition mode, then keyboard controls (arrow keys +/- modifiers) move the focal point incrementally.
2) Given a keyboard reposition action — when I finish repositioning, then the resulting focal percentage matches equivalent mouse-drag results (proportional mapping) within an acceptable tolerance defined by UX.
3) Given a completed keyboard reposition — when the action completes, then the same save call to the shared persistence API is invoked (or saved to client draft if integration story not yet done), matching the mouse save behavior.
4) Keyboard controls are announced via accessible labels and meet basic a11y keyboard navigation expectations (tab focusable, ARIA attributes present).

contents.summary: As a developer, I want resizing and proportional-placement validation for Image Widget so focal points remain correct across container width changes
contents.description:
As a developer I want automated tests and validation to ensure that stored focal percentages map proportionally when the widget or container width changes so that focal placement remains visually correct across responsive/resized states.

Acceptance Criteria:
1) Given a saved horizontal percentage focal point — when the widget is rendered at different widths (narrow, default, wide), then the visual focal point is computed and rendered proportionally and matches design expectations.
2) Given a saved focal point — when the widget is resized by user or page layout changes, then the focal mapping updates immediately without loss of proportional accuracy.
3) Include unit/integration tests that use mocked persistence responses to verify mapping logic across at least three representative breakpoints and edge cases (extreme narrow, extreme wide, and centered).
4) Document any assumptions about coordinate normalization and rounding tolerances for product/design review.

contents.summary: As a user, I want the Grouped Review 'Apply' UI (counts, tooltip, snackbar) so I can initiate bulk applies and see immediate feedback
contents.description:
As a user I want the Apply button to show item counts, display correct hover tooltip text for enabled/disabled states, and show snackbars for success/error so that I can trigger bulk apply from the UI and get feedback even if backend processing is not yet implemented.

Acceptance Criteria:
1) Given grouped items to apply — when the UI renders, then the Apply button label shows the current selected item count.
2) Given hover states — when I hover the Apply button in enabled and disabled states, then tooltip text reflects the correct state and reason.
3) Given I click Apply — when the frontend receives mock success/failure responses from a mocked backend, then the appropriate success or error snackbar appears with messages matching design guidelines.
4) The UI is implemented against a mocked API contract so it can be tested independently of backend implementation.

contents.summary: As an engineer, I want a Batch Apply backend API that processes items with batch-size limits and returns per-item success/failure so bulk applies can be executed reliably
contents.description:
As a backend engineer I want a REST API that accepts a batch apply request, enforces batch-size limits, processes items with per-item success/failure results, and returns a partial-failure-aware response so the frontend and orchestration can handle large bulk operations.

Acceptance Criteria:
1) Given a batch apply request — when the batch exceeds configured limits, then the API rejects with a 413/422 and descriptive message.
2) Given a valid batch request — when processing completes, then the response contains per-item status (success/failure) and aggregated summary (total processed, failed count).
3) The API supports idempotency keys to allow safe retries and documents expected retry semantics.
4) Provide unit tests covering full-success, partial-failure, and rejected-batch scenarios.

contents.summary: As a user, I want partial-failure handling and retry semantics surfaced so I understand which items failed and can retry selectively
contents.description:
As a user I want the system to surface partial-failure results after an Apply action and provide retry options for failed items so I can remediate and re-run only the failed subset.

Acceptance Criteria:
1) Given a partial-failure response from the backend — when Apply completes, then the UI shows a detailed result summary listing failed items and reasons in a snackbar or modal.
2) Given failed items — when I click 'Retry failed', then a new batch is created containing only failed items and sent to the backend with an idempotency key.
3) Retry behavior respects backend batch-size limits and surfaces errors if retries exceed allowed limits.
4) Provide acceptance tests that simulate partial failures and retries using the mock backend.

contents.summary: As a product owner, I want Complete Request/Stage rules implemented for mixed staged and non-staged items so completion behavior follows business rules
contents.description:
As a product owner I want the backend to apply Clear Complete Request/Stage rules when processing mixed staged and non-staged requests so that requests and stages transition correctly after bulk apply actions.

Acceptance Criteria:
1) Given a mix of staged and non-staged requests — when Apply completes, then each item transitions according to the defined rules (documented in the story) and the response indicates per-item post-action state.
2) Given staged requests at different stages — when processed, then the Complete Request/Stage behavior follows rule table (provided) and is testable via API tests.
3) Integration tests validate the combined behavior with sample datasets including mixed-stage, staged-only, and non-staged-only batches.
4) Documentation of rules and examples is included for frontend and QA teams.

contents.summary: As a backend engineer, I want job-splitting logic that creates per-folder review tasks and assigns folder-specific reviewers so cross-folder bulk edits are routed correctly
contents.description:
As a backend engineer I want the bulk-review processing to split cross-folder selections into separate review jobs per folder, attach only items from each folder to its job, and assign that folder's configured reviewers so reviewers receive only relevant items.

Acceptance Criteria:
1) Given a Cross Folder bulk edit across multiple folders — when processing starts, then the system creates one review job per folder containing only that folder's items.
2) Given folder-level reviewer configurations — when jobs are created, then each job is assigned the folder's configured reviewers (including combining global/local reviewers where applicable).
3) Unit tests cover cases where folders share a workflow template but have different reviewer sets, and verify no cross-assignment of items.
4) The API returns a summary containing per-folder job IDs and assigned reviewers for UI consumption.

contents.summary: As a backend/notification engineer, I want notification scoping to send reviewer notifications only for their folder's items so reviewers are only notified about relevant tasks
contents.description:
As a notification engineer I want the system to scope reviewer notifications to the items assigned in each per-folder job so that reviewer emails/tasks contain only the correct items and metadata.

Acceptance Criteria:
1) Given per-folder review jobs — when notifications are sent to a reviewer, then the notification includes only items from the folder(s) they are configured to review.
2) Given reviewers configured globally and locally — when a job is created, then notification logic resolves correct recipient lists and avoids duplicates.
3) Integration tests simulate creation and notification for Folder A and Folder B with distinct reviewer sets and validate delivered payloads.
4) Notification templates include per-folder metadata so reviewers can see folder context in the notification body.

contents.summary: As a Workflow Admin, I want the grouped-review UI to show per-folder segmentation and counts so I can confirm reviewers and item distribution before confirming
contents.description:
As a Workflow Admin I want the grouped-review summary UI to display separate segments per folder with item counts and assigned reviewers so I can verify review scope before confirming the grouped review.

Acceptance Criteria:
1) Given cross-folder bulk edit that results in multiple backend review jobs — when the grouped review summary shows, then it displays separate segments for each folder with item counts and assigned reviewers.
2) Given the per-folder job summary — when I expand a folder segment, then I see the list of items scoped to that folder and reviewer assignments.
3) The UI fetches per-folder job summaries from the backend contract (mocked if backend not yet available) and renders accurate counts and reviewer names.
4) Visual indicators show that items are segmented per folder and that no reviewer is assigned items outside their configured scope.

contents.summary: As a user, I want the Image Widget resizing UI and Nav Width slider two-way synced so I can resize images via drag or slider
contents.description:
As a user / I want the Image Widget to support resizing via page drag handles and a Nav Width slider / So that I can adjust the widget width with either control and see them reflect each other in real time.

Acceptance Criteria:
1) Given an Image Widget — when the user adjusts the Width slider, then the widget width changes on the page and the slider value updates in real time.
2) Given an Image Widget on the page — when the user drags the dotted outline, then the widget width updates and the Nav Width slider updates to match.
3) UX matches design tokens for dotted outline and drag handles.
4) The UI is developed against a simple agreed API contract (no persistence/algorithm implementation required in this story).

contents.summary: As a developer, I want a focal-point preservation algorithm so the image retains its horizontal percentage during resizes
contents.description:
As a developer / I want a well-tested focal-point preservation algorithm that computes and maintains an image's horizontal percentage focal point across resizes / So that images remain focused on the intended area when Block widths change or the widget is resized.

Acceptance Criteria:
1) Given an image with a stored horizontal percentage focal point — when the Block width changes or the widget is resized, then the algorithm returns pixel offsets that preserve the same horizontal percentage within the new bounds.
2) Unit tests cover boundary conditions (0%, 50%, 100%), extreme aspect-ratio changes, and rounding/precision behavior.
3) A public API surface (library function signatures and running examples) is provided so the persistence service and other widget consumers can call the algorithm.
4) Documented performance characteristics and complexity notes are provided.

contents.summary: As a service owner, I want a focal-point persistence service/API so widgets can save and load focal positions across devices
contents.description:
As a service owner / I want a small REST service that stores and returns an image's horizontal percentage focal point (per image/widget id) / So that multiple widgets and clients can persist focal positions without reimplementing storage logic.

Acceptance Criteria:
1) Implement GET /focal-point?imageId={id} and POST/PUT /focal-point with a JSON payload containing imageId and horizontalPercentage (0-100).
2) Service persists values and returns consistent results across calls; includes basic validation and error codes (200/204, 400, 404, 500).
3) Integration/unit tests validate persistence, concurrency behavior, and failure modes.
4) Service exposes a minimal, stable API surface suitable for reuse by IB2-117 and other widgets.

contents.summary: As a developer, I want a documented API contract and mock server for the focal-point service so frontends can integrate independently
contents.description:
As a developer / I want an OpenAPI/Swagger spec and a lightweight mock server for the focal-point persistence API / So that the frontend teams (and IB2-59) can implement and test integration without waiting for the production service.

Acceptance Criteria:
1) Publish an OpenAPI/Swagger spec describing GET /focal-point?imageId={id} and POST/PUT /focal-point including payload shapes, status codes, and error semantics.
2) Provide a mock server reachable on a dev URL (or container) that returns representative payloads and simulates error scenarios (404, 500, network failure) and configurable delays.
3) Documentation includes example requests/responses, retry guidance, and a compatibility checklist for frontends.
4) QA/dev teams can run the mock locally and CI tests can target it.

contents.summary: As a developer, I want to integrate the Image Widget with the focal-point API (using the published mock) so focal points persist across sessions
contents.description:
As a developer / I want the Image Widget to call the shared focal-point persistence API per the published OpenAPI contract or the provided mock server / So that the widget saves and restores horizontal percentage positions without embedding persistence logic.

Acceptance Criteria:
1) Given a saved focal point — when the widget loads, then it calls GET /focal-point?imageId={id} on the published mock and applies the returned horizontal percentage to the UI.
2) Given a reposition action — when the user finishes repositioning, then the widget issues POST/PUT to /focal-point with the agreed payload and correctly handles success and standard failure responses.
3) Given the mock or API is unavailable — the UI surfaces a non-blocking error and retries according to the documented contract; no blocking UX occurs.
4) Tests run against the published mock server to validate integration behavior.

contents.summary: As a developer, I want the Smart Pages OpenAPI spec published so teams have a stable contract for list and delete semantics
contents.description:
As a developer / I want a published OpenAPI/Swagger specification for Smart Pages endpoints / So that frontends and backend integrations have a clear contract to implement against.

Acceptance Criteria:
1) Publish an OpenAPI/Swagger spec documenting GET /smart-pages (with pagination, IDs, titles, permission-limited fields) and DELETE /smart-pages/{id} (including 204, 404, 409 semantics).
2) Include example requests/responses, pagination schema, and permission-related response variations.
3) Provide a compatibility checklist and versioning guidance so frontends know how to adopt changes.

contents.summary: As a developer, I want a Smart Pages mock server (configurable) so frontend delete/list flows can be validated independently
contents.description:
As a developer / I want a mock server implementing the Smart Pages contract / So that frontend teams and integration tests can exercise list and delete behaviors without a persistent backend.

Acceptance Criteria:
1) Mock server implements GET /smart-pages and DELETE /smart-pages/{id} per the published spec; supports empty lists, large pages, permission-limited responses.
2) DELETE responses configurable to return 204, 404, or 409 to simulate normal, not-found, and conflict scenarios.
3) Mock is reachable by frontend/dev teams (container or hosted dev URL) and includes instructions to run locally and in CI.
4) Documentation includes example scenarios for QA and integration tests.

contents.summary: As a Platform Engineer, I want multi-environment aggregation and export synchronization for weekly reports so cross-environment data can be aggregated
contents.description:
As a Platform Engineer / I want tooling to aggregate data across environments and synchronize exports with downstream reporting systems / So that reporting can combine data from multiple tenants/environments as required.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Aggregation job handles multi-environment inputs and produces combined outputs for test data sets.
- Export synchronization hooks for downstream systems are documented and exercised in a staging run.

contents.summary: As an Engineer, I want automated migration of child references and linked content when a sub-page becomes standalone so links and embeds continue to work
contents.description:
As an Engineer / I want a migration job/component that updates child references, linked content identifiers and internal links after a page is promoted to standalone / So that content integrity is preserved.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Migration updates internal references for a sample set of moved pages and produces verification logs.
- Link integrity checks report no broken links for migrated samples.
- Migration can be run as part of the move operation or as a follow-up job.

contents.summary: As an Admin, I want approval workflow hooks and role-based permission templates for sub-page changes so governance can be enforced
contents.description:
As an Admin / I want the ability to require approval workflows for permission changes and to apply role-based permission templates to sub-pages / So that permission updates follow organizational governance.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Permission change can be flagged to require approval; an approval request is created and routed to configured approvers.
- Role-based permission templates can be applied to a selected sub-page and previewed before applying.

contents.summary: As an Engineer, I want cross-platform synchronization for sub-page permission updates so mobile and API clients reflect changes promptly
contents.description:
As an Engineer / I want mechanisms to synchronize permission changes across APIs and mobile clients (websocket or pub/sub) to ensure consistent enforcement / So that permission changes are honored everywhere.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Permission updates emit change events that clients can subscribe to (sample pub/sub implementation for staging).
- A sample mobile/web client demonstrates update propagation within a small test window.

contents.summary: As a Workflow Admin, I want a toggle to Enable Grouped Requests in the workflow template UI (when MCP allows it) so grouped requests can opt into grouped notification behaviours
contents.description:
In order to let workflow authors opt-in to grouped request behavior for templates, As a Workflow Admin, I want the workflow template editor to expose an 'Enable Grouped Requests' toggle when the tenant/MCP setting allows grouped requests. When enabled, the template should surface grouped-notification options within the Requester Emails section (a defined, limited set for MVP). For estimation purposes, MVP implements: toggle visibility, persistence, and two grouped-notification checkboxes (Notify requester on grouped-start, Notify requester on grouped-complete). This story focuses on UI and config wiring; notification delivery plumbing is covered in separate stories.

Acceptance Criteria (binary Gherkin):

Scenario 1 — Toggle visibility based on MCP
Given MCP global setting "Grouped Requests Enabled" = true
When a Workflow Admin opens a workflow template editor
Then the UI shows the 'Enable Grouped Requests' toggle

Scenario 2 — Toggle persistence
Given I enable 'Enable Grouped Requests' and save the template
When I reopen the template editor
Then the toggle remains enabled and the template configuration persists

Scenario 3 — Grouped-notification options appear
Given 'Enable Grouped Requests' is enabled
When I view the Requester Emails section
Then the two MVP grouped-notification options (Notify on grouped-start, Notify on grouped-complete) are visible and can be toggled independently

contents.summary: As a developer, I want the Smart Page DELETE implemented and tested against the published mock so deletion can be verified independently
contents.description:
As a developer / I want to implement DELETE /smart-pages/{id} against the published mock server and spec / So that the UI delete flow can be verified without the persistent backend.

Acceptance Criteria:
1) Given a valid Smart Page id — when DELETE /smart-pages/{id} is called against the published mock, then the implementation returns 204 and subsequent GET calls reflect deletion per the mock's state.
2) Given a non-existent id — when DELETE is called against the mock, then the mock returns 404 and the frontend surfaces the correct error.
3) Given a conflict scenario — when DELETE is called against the mock, then the mock returns 409 and the system surfaces conflict messaging per design.
4) Integration tests validate delete behavior against the published mock server and reference the exact spec+mock used.
5) If the published mock is not available, the story includes delivering a temporary lightweight mock for test automation (documented and removed once the published mock is available).
